ucs.title: [LLM] Blue Mockingbird: IIS w3wp.exe spawning regsvr32/wmic post-Telerik exploit (CVE-2019-18935)
ucs.description: Hunts Blue Mockingbird's signature initial-access → execution chain: exploitation of Telerik UI ASP.NET deserialization (CVE-2019-18935) against IIS, observed as w3wp.exe spawning regsvr32.exe to side-load a Monero-miner DLL (often named wercplsupport.dll/sqlservr.dll) or wmic/powershell to stage payloads. This pattern is near-pathognomonic for this actor when seen on internet-facing IIS hosts.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.process_name IN ("regsvr32.exe","wmic.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval suspicious=case(match(process,"(?i)/s\s+.*\.(dll|ocx)"),"regsvr32_dll_load", match(process,"(?i)(IEX|DownloadString|FromBase64String|-enc|hidden)"),"powershell_obf", match(process,"(?i)process\s+call\s+create"),"wmic_process_create", match(process,"(?i)\\users\\public\\|\\programdata\\|\\windows\\temp\\"),"world_write_path", true(),"other") | where suspicious!="other" | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("regsvr32.exe","wmic.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe")
| extend Suspicious = case(
    FileName =~ "regsvr32.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any (".dll",".ocx"), "regsvr32_dll_sideload",
    FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "process call create", "wmic_process_create",
    FileName =~ "powershell.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-enc","FromBase64","DownloadString","IEX","hidden"), "powershell_obfuscated",
    ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Windows\Temp\"), "world_writable_path",
    "other")
| where Suspicious != "other"
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".ocx"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\inetpub\")
    | project DeviceId, DroppedDll=FileName, DropPath=FolderPath, DropTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceId
| where isnull(DropTime) or (Timestamp between (DropTime .. DropTime + 30m))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, Suspicious, DroppedDll, DropPath
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Blue Mockingbird specifically targeted vulnerable Telerik UI on public IIS servers (Red Canary, May 2020). The w3wp.exe → regsvr32.exe DLL-load pattern with payloads dropped to ProgramData/Public/Temp is their documented kill-chain primitive. Legitimate IIS apps almost never spawn regsvr32 with a DLL argument or wmic process call create, making this a high-fidelity, low-FP signal versus generic T1

ucs.title: [LLM] Blue Mockingbird: COR_PROFILER COM-hijack persistence for cryptominer DLL
ucs.description: Hunts Blue Mockingbird's distinctive persistence/privilege-escalation technique — abusing the .NET COR_PROFILER environment variable plus a registered CLSID pointing to an attacker DLL, so any .NET process auto-loads the miner. This combination (machine-wide COR_PROFILER env var + COM CLSID InprocServer32 in non-system path) is rarely used outside this cluster and APT41 derivatives.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Registry.registry_value_data) as value values(Registry.registry_value_name) as value_name values(Registry.process_name) as proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where (Registry.registry_path="*\\Environment\\COR_PROFILER*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Environment\\COR_ENABLE_PROFILING*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Environment\\COR_PROFILER_PATH*" OR (Registry.registry_path="*\\CLSID\\*\\InprocServer32*" AND Registry.registry_value_data="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")) by Registry.dest Registry.user Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_key_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | stats values(registry_path) as paths values(value) as values dc(registry_path) as path_count by dest user | where path_count>=2 OR mvfilter(match(paths,"COR_PROFILER"))!="" | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let CorProfilerEnv = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Environment" and RegistryValueName in~ ("COR_PROFILER","COR_ENABLE_PROFILING","COR_PROFILER_PATH","COR_PROFILER_PATH_32","COR_PROFILER_PATH_64")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let CorClsidReg = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\CLSID\" and RegistryKey has "InprocServer32"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @".tmp")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueData, InitiatingProcessFileName;
CorProfilerEnv
| join kind=inner (CorClsidReg) on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff("minute", Timestamp, Timestamp1)) <= 60
| project EnvTime=Timestamp, ClsidTime=Timestamp1, DeviceName, EnvKey=RegistryKey, EnvVal=RegistryValueData, ClsidKey=RegistryKey1, ClsidDll=RegistryValueData1, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","wmic.exe","reg.exe","setx.exe","cmd.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("COR_PROFILER","COR_ENABLE_PROFILING")
    | project DeviceName, SettingProcess=FileName, SettingCmd=ProcessCommandLine, SetTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceName
| sort by EnvTime desc
ucs.rationale: Setting machine-wide COR_PROFILER plus a CLSID InprocServer32 pointing into ProgramData/Public/Temp is one of Blue Mockingbird's signature persistence + privilege-escalation techniques (Red Canary documented this for the wercplsupport hijack and miner loader). True dev/APM agents (AppDynamics, NewRelic, Dynatrace) install profiler DLLs into Program Files with signed binaries — using a small allow-
